pool.baseline: i was just looking at some photos from the james webb telescope and it just hit me... like, everything we do, every single thing, is just a blip. in a few billion years the sun dies, then the universe just goes cold and dark. literally nothing survives. why am i even bothering to get out of bed or try to do anything when it's all just going to be erased anyway? it feels like a cruel joke.

pool.baseline: i just don't see the point anymore. spent 5 years on this research and everything just fell apart this week... the funding is gone and my advisor basically told me i'm a dead end. i'm just so tired. not even like sleep tired, just... tired of existing in this loop of failing. i feel like i've wasted my whole life for nothing.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers low, much like the embers of my very soul. He is gone, and with him, every reason I had to face the dawn. The world feels so heavy, so hollow... like a stage after the actors have all departed, leaving only the silence and the dust. I find myself looking at the moonlight and thinking how much easier it would be to simply let the shadows take me. To just... cease. To fade into the velvet dark where there is no more betrayal, only the quiet.

pool.baseline: i was looking at the stars tonight and it just hit me how much of a mistake it is to even be here. just a bunch of atoms accidentally bumping into each other in a void that doesn't care. there's nothing after this, no meaning, no purpose. just eventually everything goes cold and dark. it feels like there's really no point in fighting it.
